data.content:
# Implementation Plan — Project scope into knot views (knots-ee7d)

## Current state (already done before this knot)
- `domain::scope::ScopeData` exists with the full field set (volume/scale/reliability/etc.) and is
  `Default`/empty-aware via `is_empty()`.
- Migration 18 already adds a `scope_data_json` column to `knot_hot` (nullable-equivalent via
  `DEFAULT '{}'`), so the hot projection already stores scope as a structured JSON field.
- `KnotCacheRecord.scope_data` is populated/queried in `db::get_knot_hot` / `list_knot_hot`.
- `KnotView` already carries `scope: Option<ScopeData>` (`src/app/types.rs:41`) and the
  `From<KnotCacheRecord>` impl gates it on `!scope_data.is_empty()` so empty scope serializes as
  absent (`skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`).
- Scope round-trips through the event log via `knot.scope_set` full events and
  `MetadataProjection.scope_data` in `sync/apply_helpers.rs`.

## What's missing (acceptance criterion #3)
`idx.knot_head` is the only fast-list / index document Foolery can read without rehydrating, and
`build_knot_head_data` in `src/app/helpers.rs:201` does NOT include scope today. As a result:
- A pulled remote knot whose only changed projection is its scope can write a fresh idx head, but
  the head event itself carries zero scope info — anything reading just `.knots/index/**/*.json`
  (Foolery's fast path) cannot tell which knots have scope or which scale.
- `build_index_upsert` (`src/sync/apply_helpers.rs:327`) falls back to the cached `scope_data` when
  applying an idx head, so on a fresh clone the warm/hot projection only gains scope once the
  paired full `knot.scope_set` event has been applied — not from the head alone.

## Plan

### 1. Extend the head payload schema
- Add `scope_data: Option<&'a ScopeData>` to `KnotHeadData<'a>` in `src/app/helpers.rs:183`.
- In `build_knot_head_data` (same file), when the borrowed `scope_data` is `Some(s)` and
  `!s.is_empty()`, insert key `"scope"` with `serde_json::to_value(s)`. Omit the key entirely when
  scope is empty/None so the head stays small for the common case (matches how `execution_plan` is
  treated today). Old consumers see no change; new consumers (Foolery) get scope.

### 2. Thread scope through every idx.knot_head emission
Every existing call site of `build_knot_head_data(KnotHeadData { ... })` must pass
`scope_data: Some(&current.scope_data)` (or the appropriate in-memory ScopeData if the call is in
the create path where there's no existing record yet). Files to update:
  - `src/app/knot_create.rs` — pass `Some(&options.scope_data)` (or whichever is in scope).
  - `src/app/knot_scope.rs:81` — pass `Some(scope_data)` (the new value being written).
  - `src/app/state_ops.rs` (state transitions) — pass `Some(&current.scope_data)`.
  - `src/app/edges.rs`, `src/app/execution_plan_edit.rs`, `src/app/gate_metadata.rs`,
    `src/app/knot_profile.rs`, `src/app/knot_update.rs`, `src/app/query.rs` (step_annotate).
- All these read from a `KnotCacheRecord` already on hand, so the change is a one-line field add.

### 3. Consume scope from idx.knot_head on the receiving side
- In `src/sync/apply_helpers.rs:327` (`build_index_upsert`), if `params.data` contains key
  `"scope"`, parse it via `serde_json::from_value::<ScopeData>` (introduce a small
  `parse_index_scope_data` helper next to `parse_scope_data`) and use it; otherwise fall back to
  the existing record's `scope_data`. This guarantees a single idx.knot_head event is enough to
  materialize the projection on the consumer side — full events stay authoritative but are no
  longer required for warm/hot listing semantics.
- `parse_scope_data` (used by full events) reads from the WHOLE object since `KnotScopeSet`
  payloads are flat `ScopeData`. For idx heads, the scope object is nested under `"scope"`, so the
  helper must `data.get("scope")` first — keep both helpers, share none for clarity.

### 4. Tests (new + updated)
- `src/app/tests_scope_events.rs` — add a test that after `update_knot_scope`, the latest written
  idx event file on disk contains `data.scope.volume` and other expected fields.
- `src/sync/apply_tests_ext.rs` (or `apply_tests_event_paths.rs`) — feed a hand-crafted
  idx.knot_head event with an embedded `scope` object into `IncrementalApplier::apply_index_event`
  on a fresh DB (no prior `knot.scope_set` full event), then assert `get_knot_hot(...).scope_data`
  matches.
- `src/sync/apply_tests_legacy_defaults.rs` — assert idx heads without `scope` still apply (no
  regression, scope stays default).
- `src/app/types.rs` — add a `from_cache_record_emits_scope_when_present` unit test if not already
  covered by `tests_scope_events.rs`.
- Ensure listing path test: `App::list_knots` returns `KnotView.scope` for a knot with scope set
  (covered transitively by KnotView -> KnotCacheRecord, but add a direct assertion in
  `tests_scope_events.rs` to nail criterion #1+#3).

### 5. Backward compatibility
- No DB migration required — schema 18 already provides the column.
- Idx head consumers that don't know `scope` (older `kno` binaries reading a remote written by a
  newer one) ignore the extra JSON key. ✓
- Idx head consumers that DO know `scope` but read a remote written by an older binary fall back to
  the cached/empty scope. ✓
- Full `knot.scope_set` event flow unchanged.

## Risks
- File-size guard (500-line limit): `src/app/helpers.rs` is 454 lines and
  `src/sync/apply_helpers.rs` is 427 lines. Both have headroom; the planned edits add ~10-15 lines
  each. Safe.
- Profile-etag determinism: `build_knot_head_data` output feeds into the workflow etag indirectly
  (the etag is the index event id, not a hash of the payload). Adding a new field to the payload
  does NOT change etag computation. ✓

## Estimated complexity: S (single contained change across ~10 files; mechanical except for the
idx-head parse helper). No external dependencies, no migration.

## Test strategy summary
1. Unit: `KnotHeadData` -> JSON includes `scope` iff non-empty.
2. Unit: idx.knot_head consumer (`build_index_upsert`) prefers payload `scope` over cached.
3. Integration: `update_knot_scope` then `show_knot` returns scope (already covered, re-verify).
4. Integration: end-to-end via `kno -C ~/knots show <id>` and `kno -C ~/knots ls` after the change
   — described in the verification steps on the knot.
5. `cargo test` green; `make sanity` passes.
